subject: [PATCH v4 1/2] mm/vmstat, mm/memcontrol: add _monotonic vmstat readers

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch adds new functions to the Linux kernel that allow reading monotonic vmstat counters without clamping negative values. This is necessary for certain use cases where a snapshot of an event counter is taken and then compared to a later value, as clamping can cause incorrect results when the underlying signed long wraps past its maximum value. The new functions return the underlying state value cast to unsigned long, allowing for correct modular subtraction across signed-long wraparounds.

2026-07-23.patch_summary: This patch adds monotonic versions of vmstat readers to handle monotonically-incremented event counters correctly across signed-long wraparound. The existing clamped helpers are kept for non-monotonic page-count readers, which can still present transient negative reads as zero. The new _monotonic variants return the underlying state value cast to unsigned long, ensuring correct results when computing deltas between two samples. This patch is a prerequisite for replacing producer-side accumulators with read-side accumulators that sample monotonic per-LRU vmstat counters.
